title: dryas octopetala subsp. viscosa
intro: Dryas octopetala subsp. viscosa, also known as the sticky dryad or sticky mountain avens, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects due to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Dryas octopetala subsp. viscosa include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health concerns. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to address a range of health issues.
